scope_note: QA Particle Mass Cosmos Orbit Cert [290]. Primary sources: (1) CODATA 2018 (Mohr, Newell, Taylor, Tiesinga). DOI: 10.1103/RevModPhys.93.025010 — particle masses in electron-mass units. (2) Particle Data Group (2022). Review of Particle Physics. PTEP 2022, 083C01. DOI: 10.1093/ptep/ptac097. (3) Briddell, D. (2024). Field Structure Theory (FST values). Mechanism: QA Orbit Access Theorem cert [279]; divisor-shortcut orbit classifier (exact for m=24, gcd(m,5)=1): singularity iff b==m AND e==m; satellite iff (m//3)|b AND (m//3)|e; cosmos otherwise. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For every standard particle mass M in electron-mass units (integer-rounded, CODATA 2018) and every FST constellation value, every Pythagorean triple (d,e) with d²-e²=M (F-interpretation) or 2de=M (C-interpretation) has orbit class cosmos under qa_step mod 24. The ≡2 mod 4 values (kaon+, kaon0, sigma0, alpha, structor, top-cluster, 2x729, 2xlambda) have zero F-triples but ≥1 C-triples, completing the coverage. Proton (1836) has zero primitive F-triples and ≥1 primitive C-triples — it is C-primary. Singularity and satellite blockade is total: 0 violations across 38 distinct mass values and 100+ triples. Theorem NT compliance: integer arithmetic throughout; orbit class by exact integer divisibility; no float feedback. Scope: does NOT certify continuous mass values (only integer-rounded); does NOT cover W/Z/Higgs (masses >10^5 mₑ, factorization outside trial-division range of this validator); does NOT certify neutrino masses (sub-electron-mass scale).
